intro: Declieuxia chiococcoides, also known as "Guanacaste," is a plant species of the Rubiaceae family native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Guanacaste plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, boasting various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Declieuxia chiococcoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The Guanacaste plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tea in traditional herbal medicine, and it is also used in the production of extracts and tinctures for various applications.
